merged_events.event_description: The first U.S. Consul-General to Nuku'alofa, Church Howe, was appointed. The post was resident in Apia, Samoa, making this a nonresident consular relationship.

evidence.quote: The first U.S. Consul-General to Nuku'alofa, Church Howe, was appointed on June 30, 1897 and replaced by Luther W. Osborn on July 26, 1897. The post was resident in Apia, Samoa, and closed in 1901.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. consular post to Nuku'alofa, resident in Apia, Samoa, closed in 1901, ending U.S. diplomatic representation in Tonga. This coincided with Tonga becoming a British protectorate in 1900 and the U.S. ceasing to appoint consul-generals.

evidence.quote: The post was resident in Apia, Samoa, and closed in 1901.
In 1900 when Tonga became a protectorate of Great Britain, the British Foreign Office undertook control over all Tongan external affairs, and the U.S. ceased to appoint consul-generals to Nuku'alofa.

merged_events.event_description: The United States accredited its Ambassador to New Zealand, Fiji, and Western Samoa, Kenneth Franzheim II, to Tonga. Franzheim presented his credentials on November 6, 1972, establishing nonresident ambassadorial relations with Tonga.

evidence.quote: On October 4, 1972, the United States accredited its Ambassador to New Zealand, Fiji, and Western Samoa, Kenneth Franzheim II, to Tonga; however, Franzheim resigned from his post in Wellington before he presented his credentials to Tonga on November 6, 1972.

merged_events.event_description: The U.S. Ambassador to Fiji (resident in Suva) replaced the U.S. Ambassador to New Zealand as the nonresident ambassador accredited to Tonga. John P. Condon presented his credentials to the Tongan government on April 11, 1979.

evidence.quote: Beginning in 1979, the U.S. Ambassador to Fiji has also been accredited to Tonga, resident in Suva. On April 11, 1979, John P. Condon presented his credentials to the Tongan government.

merged_events.event_description: The United States officially opened the U.S. Embassy in Nuku'alofa, the Tongan capital, elevating the diplomatic relationship from a nonresident ambassadorship to a resident embassy.

evidence.quote: On May 9, 2023, the United States officially opened the U.S. Embassy in Nuku'alofa.

matched.notes: Exact date and status match. Both reference appointment of Church Howe as first U.S. Consul-General to Nuku'alofa on June 30, 1897, resident in Apia, Samoa.
Both give year 1901 and status None. Extracted event provides no more precise date than the year. Refers to closure of the consular post in Apia.
Exact date (1972-11-06) and status match. Both reference the credential presentation of Kenneth Franzheim II to Tonga establishing nonresident ambassadorial relations.
Exact date (2023-05-09) and status match. Both reference the official opening of the U.S. Embassy in Nuku'alofa.

missing_from_csv.event_description: The U.S. Ambassador to Fiji (resident in Suva) replaced the U.S. Ambassador to New Zealand as the nonresident ambassador accredited to Tonga. John P. Condon presented credentials on April 11, 1979.
missing_from_csv.notes: The diplomatic status remains Ambassador Nonresident, so this is not a status transition per se. However, it represents a significant change in the residency arrangement (from Wellington/NZ-based to Suva/Fiji-based). RDCR explicitly notes this shift ('Beginning in 1979, the U.S. Ambassador to Fiji has also been accredited to Tonga, resident in Suva'). Consider adding if the dataset tracks changes in nonresident residency location, otherwise it can be omitted as the status category does not change.
